---
title: "Assignment 2"
date: "January 20, 2026"
date-modified: "last-modified"
format: html
editor: visual
---

::: callout-note
## Creating data visualisation that is enligthening and truthful

Creating enlightening and truthful data visualizations involves focusing on accuracy, transparency, and the ability to effectively communicate insights. It's about presenting data in a way that is both informative and aesthetically pleasing, ensuring the audience can grasp the information quickly and accurately.
:::

## Overview

This handout provides the context, task, expectations and grading criteria of Take-home Exercise 2. Students must review and understand them before getting started with the take-home exercise.

## Setting the scene

A local online media company that publishes daily content on digital platforms is planning to release an article on **The Dynamics of Demographic Structures and Geographic Distribution of Singapore Population since 2020**.

## The Task

Assume the role of a graphical editor at a media company. You are required to design **no more than six data visualisations** to support and enhance the accompanying article. The visualisations should be clear, engaging, and appropriate for a general audience.

### Assignment Requirements

Your submission must include:

- At least one data visualisation that provides a high-level overview of overall population trends.
- At least one data visualisation that reveals the dynamics of the demographic structure by age cohort and gender.
- At least one data visualisation that illustrates the geographic distribution of demographic structures.

### Design and Analytical Expectations

In addition to meeting the minimum visualisation requirements, you are expected to:

- Select visualisation types that are appropriate for the data and the intended message.
- Apply sound visual design principles (e.g., clarity, accuracy, appropriate scales, effective use of colour, and proper labelling).
- Ensure that each visualisation communicates a clear and focused insight rather than merely presenting data.
- Avoid redundancy across visualisations; each chart should contribute a distinct perspective.

## The Data

To accomplish the task, *Singapore Residents by Planning Area / Subzone, Age Group, Sex and Type of Dwelling* shares by [Department of Statistics, Singapore (DOS)](https://www.singstat.gov.sg/) must be used.

## The Designing Tool

The data visualisation must be prepared by using **Tableau Desktop Professional Edition** and published on **Tableau Public**.

## The Write-up

The write-up of the take-home exercise should include but not limited to the followings:

-   A reproducible description of the procedures used to prepare the analytical visualisation.  It must also justifying the choice of visualisation type and design principles used.
-   A write-up of no more than **150 words** per each data visualisation, describing and discussing the patterns reveal by each visualisation prepared.

## Grading Criteria

This assignment will be graded by using the following criteria:

-   Data wrangling (20 marks): This is an important aspect of visual analytics. You will be assessed on your ability to employ appropriate Tableau functions to import, extract, clean, transform and process data. Besides assessing your ability to use the Tableau functions, this criterion also includes your ability to clean and derive appropriate variables to meet the visual analysis need.

-   Visual Analysis (30 marks): In this exercise, you are expected to utilise the visual analytics methods and design best practices introduced in class, along with the Tableau methods learned during the in-class exercises, to perform your analysis. You will be assessed on your ability to apply these methods correctly and to provide accurate interpretations and discussions of the analysis results.

-   Visual Communication (20 marks): In this section, your ability to effectively communicate visual analysis results through user-friendly visual representations will be assessed. Since this course is focused on visual analysis, it is crucial that you demonstrate proficiency in using appropriate visualization techniques to clearly convey the findings of your analysis.

-   Reproducibility (15 marks): This is a key learning outcome of this course. You will be assessed on your ability to thoroughly document the data visualisation design procedures.

-   Bonus (15 marks): Demonstrate your ability to employ methods beyond what you had learned in class to gain insights from the data. The methods used must be visual analytics in nature.

## Submission Instructions

This is an individual assignment. You are required to work on the assignment and prepare submission individually.

Students are required to The specific submission instructions are as follows:

-   Publish the data visualisation report on Tableau Pulic and provide the url on eLearn. The report must be in Tableau **Story** format.
-   Upload the Tableau Workbook of the assignment submission on eLearn, and
-   upload the MS Word version of the write-up of the assignment on eLearn. It should include but not limited to the [write-up](https://va4bi-ay2025-26t2-g2.netlify.app/assign/assign1#the-task) requirements above.

### Submission date

The completed take-home exercise is due on **15th March  2026 (Sunday) by 11:59pm mid-night**.

## Reference

- [Demographic Structure](https://fiveable.me/key-terms/ap-hug/demographic-structure)
- [Population pyramid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Population_pyramid)
- [Geographical Distribution Dashboard](https://www.singstat.gov.sg/find-data/search-by-theme/population/geographic-distribution/visualising-data/geographical-distribution-dashboard)
